title: Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity)
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming nearly every aspect of modern life—from education and healthcare to governance, journalism, business, and communication. As AI systems become increasingly integrated into society, there is an urgent need for experts who can critically examine how these technologies influence humanity, ethics, public policy, culture, and everyday life. To help address these challenges, the Center for Humane Technology (CHT) has launched its inaugural Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship , a prestigious six-month remote fellowship designed to support interdisciplinary professionals who are committed to advancing thoughtful conversations and impactful research on AI and society. The fellowship offers a $30,000 stipend , extensive mentorship, editorial support, media training, and an opportunity to become part of a growing community of experts shaping the future of responsible AI. If you are a mid-career researcher, policy professional, journalist, advocate, technologist, or thought leader interested in AI s societal impacts, this fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity to amplify your work while contributing to one of the world s most important conversations. Application Deadline: July 12, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST About the Center for Humane Technology (CHT) The Center for Humane Technology (CHT) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to ensuring that technology serves humanity s best interests. Rather than focusing solely on technological advancement, CHT works to understand how emerging technologies influence individuals, communities, institutions, and society as a whole. The organization aims to: Clarify the societal impacts of rapidly evolving technologies. Connect everyday technological harms to larger systemic issues. Promote responsible technology development. Encourage informed public conversations. Support practical solutions that align innovation with human values. Recognizing that these complex challenges require diverse perspectives, CHT established the Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship to empower experts from multiple disciplines to contribute meaningful research, public engagement, and innovative ideas. Fellowship Overview The Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship is a six-month remote program that supports professionals exploring critical questions about AI s impact on society. Fellowship Details Fellowship Type: Remote Duration: September 14, 2026 – March 18, 2027 Commitment: 15 hours per week Required overlap: At least 10 hours during Eastern Standard Time (EST) Fellowship Cohort: 3 Fellows Stipend: $30,000 Payment Structure: Paid upon completion of three program milestones Fellowship Focus Areas Applicants may apply to one or two of the following research areas. 1. AI and Cognition This area examines how AI affects: Human thinking Learning Decision-making Critical thinking Intellectual independence Creativity Cognitive development in adults and children Diversity of thought Human-machine collaboration Researchers are encouraged to investigate whether AI merely enhances human capabilities or fundamentally changes the way people think. 2. AI and Relationships This theme explores how artificial intelligence influences: Human relationships Social interaction Communication Emotional wellbeing Community building Human connection 3. AI and Surveillance Applicants may investigate topics including: Privacy Data collection Digital monitoring Government surveillance Corporate surveillance Civil liberties Human rights 4. AI and Spirituality Potential research areas include: Human meaning Identity Faith Values Purpose Spiritual wellbeing The philosophical implications of AI 5. Inside the Machine: AI from a Technologist s Perspective Ideal for professionals with technical backgrounds interested in explaining: AI system development Model design Engineering decisions Technical trade-offs Industry practices AI infrastructure Responsible AI development Fellowship Expectations Selected fellows will actively contribute to CHT s public engagement and research initiatives. Each fellow will be expected to: Complete one flagship research output such as: Research paper Long-form essay Public report Produce between one and three opinion articles (op-eds) for major publications or CHT s Substack. Participate in a podcast episode on Your Undivided Attention or another CHT platform where appropriate. Contribute to CHT s media and communications ecosystem. Attend: Three mandatory cohort meetings. Mandatory bi-weekly meetings with the Senior Director of Strategy and Impact. Fellowship Benefits Participants receive far more than financial support. Benefits include: Financial Support $30,000 stipend Milestone-based payments Professional Mentorship Bi-weekly guidance from CHT s Senior Director of Strategy and Impact Research support from the CHT Research Team Communications Support Editorial assistance Graphic design support Communications support Media training Public relations assistance Media placement opportunities Professional Visibility Fellows will also receive: A CHT email account Access to selected CHT Slack channels Public profile on the CHT Fellows webpage Opportunities to publish work reaching global audiences Who Should Apply? The fellowship welcomes professionals from interdisciplinary backgrounds, including but not limited to: Researchers Academics Junior faculty members Policy analysts Former government officials Journalists Writers Civil society advocates Community organizers Technology professionals AI governance experts Digital rights specialists Platform accountability experts Professionals studying technology s social impacts Relevant academic backgrounds may include: Psychology Sociology Philosophy Cognitive Science Communications Science and Technology Studies Ideal Candidate Profile Successful applicants should demonstrate: Research Excellence Applicants should have: Strong analytical skills Proven research experience Ability to deliver high-quality work on schedule Experience producing public-facing research Communication Skills Strong candidates should be able to: Explain complex ideas clearly Write for broad audiences Speak publicly Engage through social media or other communication platforms An existing audience is helpful but not required . Perspective on AI Applicants should understand: AI s societal impacts Technology governance Digital rights Policy development Human-centered AI The influence of business incentives on technology They should also demonstrate: Intellectual honesty Critical thinking Nuanced analysis Commitment to practical impact Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following: A 1–2 page research proposal for each selected topic (up to two topics). A CV or résumé (maximum two pages). A two-minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you are a strong candidate. A writing sample demonstrating research and analytical ability. Why You Should Apply This fellowship provides an exceptional platform for professionals seeking to influence one of today s most important global conversations. Participants will gain: Financial support National and international visibility Professional mentorship Media exposure Editorial support Opportunities to publish influential work Networking with interdisciplinary experts Experience contributing to responsible AI policy and public discourse Whether your expertise lies in research, journalism, policymaking, technology, or advocacy, the Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to shape how artificial intelligence develops in ways that benefit humanity. Important Dates Activity Date Applications Close July 12, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Fellowship Begins September 14, 2026 Fellowship Ends March 18, 2027 How to Apply Interested applicants should submit all required application materials before the deadline through the official application portal. Click here for more and to apply For additional information about the fellowship and the Center for Humane Technology, visit the official fellowship page. Click here for more global Fellowship opportunities The post Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026: Win a $10,000 Non-Dilutive Stipend, Expert Mentorship, Investor Access, and Climate Week NYC Participation
summary: The New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from early-stage climate innovators around the world. Designed to bridge the critical gap between breakthrough research and market-ready solutions, this prestigious six-month hybrid fellowship equips researchers, scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and innovators with the tools, mentorship, funding, and industry connections needed to accelerate climate technologies that strengthen energy systems and urban resilience. Applications are now open and will be reviewed on a rolling basis until Saturday, August 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM . Supported by the New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA) , the 2026 fellowship builds on the success of its inaugural 2025 cohort and focuses specifically on innovations at the intersection of Energy and Urban Resilience . Participants will receive structured commercialization support, a non-dilutive stipend, one-on-one mentoring, investor introductions, and the opportunity to participate in Climate Week NYC , one of the world s premier climate events. What Is the Climate Tech Fellowship? The Climate Tech Fellowship is a six-month hybrid accelerator programme developed by The New York Climate Exchange to help promising climate technologies move beyond the research stage and become commercially viable solutions. Many groundbreaking climate innovations never reach the marketplace because innovators often lack: Commercialization expertise Market strategy Customer discovery experience Investor connections Mentorship Venture-building support This challenge is commonly referred to as the first valley of death —the difficult transition between research and successful commercialization. The fellowship was created specifically to bridge this gap by helping innovators transform promising ideas into scalable climate solutions capable of creating real-world impact. Mission of the Fellowship The programme aims to accelerate climate innovation by supporting innovators who are developing technologies capable of addressing urgent global climate challenges. Rather than offering funding alone, the fellowship combines financial support with structured business development, commercialization guidance, technical mentoring, and access to a powerful global innovation ecosystem. Participants leave the programme with: A clearer commercialization roadmap Greater fundraising readiness Stronger investor relationships Expanded professional networks Improved market positioning Long-term mentorship Focus Area for the 2026 Fellowship The 2026 fellowship focuses exclusively on: Energy Urban Resilience Cities face increasing risks from: Extreme heat Flooding Climate-related disasters Energy disruptions Infrastructure failures The fellowship seeks innovations that improve how cities prepare for, respond to, and recover from climate-related challenges while supporting the clean energy transition. Innovations may focus on—but are not limited to—the following areas: 1. End-use Energy Innovation Examples include: Intelligent buildings Building energy efficiency Smart infrastructure Energy management systems 2. Power Generation Storage Projects involving: Renewable generation Battery technologies Distributed energy systems Energy storage innovations 3. Grid Modernization Solutions addressing: Smart grids Power system monitoring Grid communications Adaptive electricity management Grid resilience 4. Natural Carbon Solutions Innovations related to: Green infrastructure Carbon storage Nature-based solutions Sustainable land management Cross-cutting innovations spanning multiple focus areas are also encouraged. Applicants uncertain about their project s fit are encouraged to apply or contact the programme team. What Fellows Receive Selected fellows gain access to an extensive package of financial, technical, and professional support. $10,000 Non-Dilutive Funding Each selected team receives: A $10,000 non-dilutive stipend Importantly, participants do not give up equity in exchange for funding. Commercialization Curriculum Participants receive structured training covering: Market mapping Customer segmentation Commercialization strategy Go-to-market planning Target market identification Sales channels Venture development Expert Sessions The fellowship also includes specialized sessions on: Fundraising Intellectual property (IP) Climate equity Communications strategy Personalized Mentorship Every fellow receives: Dedicated one-on-one mentoring Mentors matched to the fellow s technical area Guidance aligned with stage of development Customized Fellowship Blueprint Participants receive: Individualized commercialization roadmap Monthly progress check-ins Tailored milestone planning Climate Week NYC Participation One of the fellowship highlights is participation in Climate Week NYC . Selected fellows receive: In-person programming Investor showcase opportunities Partner engagement Networking sessions Travel and accommodation for one team member are fully covered. Global Network Access Participants also benefit from introductions to: Investors Policymakers Strategic partners Climate organizations Innovation ecosystem leaders Programme Timeline The fellowship runs from September 2026 to February 2027 . September 2026 Programme onboarding Climate Week NYC participation October 2026 – January 2027 Virtual programming including: Commercialization modules Mentor meetings Cohort sessions Customer discovery Venture development February 2027 Virtual showcase Programme wrap-up Who Should Apply? The fellowship is intended for early-stage innovators developing technologies that strengthen energy systems and urban resilience. Ideal applicants include: Researchers Scientists Engineers Entrepreneurs Startup founders Applicants should ideally be: At prototype stage At early pilot stage At early commercialization stage The fellowship is particularly valuable for innovators seeking commercialization support rather than research funding alone. University affiliation is not required , and applicants do not need to have incorporated a company . The programme is designed for innovations from the pre-company formation to pre-seed stage . Applications from founders from underrepresented backgrounds are especially encouraged. Eligibility Criteria Applicants should meet the following criteria: Be an early-stage innovator, researcher, scientist, engineer, founder, or entrepreneur. Be developing an innovation related to Energy and Urban Resilience . Have a prototype, early pilot, or early commercialization-stage solution. Be interested in commercialization, customer discovery, and venture building. Commit several hours per week throughout the six-month programme. Be available for virtual sessions and in-person participation during Climate Week NYC. Be willing to focus on one innovation throughout the fellowship. Notably: University affiliation is not required . Company incorporation is not required . Teams may apply. Applicants from any country are eligible. Application Process Applications are completed online and are free of charge. Applicants should submit details about: Their innovation Team members Fellowship goals Commercialization plans Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , so early submission is strongly encouraged. The review process includes: Eligibility screening Completeness check Evaluation of commercialization readiness Assessment of impact potential Alignment with the 2026 focus area Virtual interviews for shortlisted applicants Final selection and onboarding APPLY HERE Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Is the fellowship free? Yes. There is no application fee and participation is free for selected fellows. Is the fellowship open internationally? Yes. The programme is open to innovators globally . Do I need to live in New York? No. The fellowship is primarily virtual. Fellows only travel to New York during Climate Week NYC. Do I need to be affiliated with a university? No. Independent innovators are welcome to apply. Do I need to have a registered company? No. Incorporation is not required. The fellowship specifically supports innovators before company formation or at the pre-seed stage. Can teams apply? Yes. Teams are welcome. However, each team receives one $10,000 stipend, and travel support is provided for one team member. Can I apply if my innovation is still in research? Yes. The programme supports research-stage innovations provided applicants are committed to commercialization. Can I apply with multiple projects? Yes, but applicants should focus on one specific innovation during the fellowship. How many fellows will be selected? The Exchange expects to select between five and ten teams for the 2026 cohort. Why This Fellowship Matters Climate innovation often stalls before reaching the market due to limited access to commercialization expertise, strategic partnerships, and investment opportunities. The Climate Tech Fellowship directly addresses these barriers by combining financial support with mentorship, expert guidance, investor exposure, and structured venture development. For innovators working at the intersection of clean energy and urban resilience, the programme provides an exceptional platform to refine technologies, strengthen commercialization strategies, and build lasting relationships within the global climate innovation ecosystem. Visit HERE to learn more about the New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026. Conclusion The New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for early-stage climate innovators worldwide to transform promising ideas into commercially viable solutions. Through a six-month hybrid programme, fellows receive a $10,000 non-dilutive stipend, personalized mentorship, commercialization training, investor introductions, and the opportunity to showcase their innovations during Climate Week NYC. Whether you are a researcher, engineer, scientist, entrepreneur, or founder developing solutions in energy, grid modernization, power storage, natural carbon solutions, or urban resilience, this fellowship offers the resources and network needed to accelerate your innovation toward real-world impact. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, but the final deadline is Saturday, August 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM . Early applications are encouraged to maximize your chances of selection. Discover more global Fellowship , Tech , and Climate Action opportunities on OFY. title: CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026)
summary: The CIVICUS Global Alliance is offering multiple high-impact opportunities in 2026 for researchers, consultants, evaluators, and interns interested in civic space, human rights monitoring, data analysis, and global civil society strengthening. These roles support the CIVICUS Monitor, one of the world’s leading platforms tracking civic freedoms across nearly 200 countries and territories. These openings are ideal for candidates passionate about democracy, human rights, international relations, political science, data coding, and global development. They also contribute directly to global reports such as the People Power Under Attack annual publication and other civic space monitoring initiatives. About CIVICUS Global Alliance CIVICUS is a global alliance of civil society organizations working in over 180 countries. Its mission is to strengthen citizen participation, defend civic freedoms, and amplify voices from the Global South. Through its CIVICUS Monitor, the organization documents violations of freedoms of association, peaceful assembly, and expression while producing global civic space ratings and analytical reports used by policymakers, researchers, and civil society actors worldwide. 1. Civic Space Research Intern (2026) Overview of the Role The Civic Space Research Intern will support the CIVICUS Monitor team in strengthening global research collaboration ahead of the People Power Under Attack report. The intern will contribute to: Research on civic space incidents globally Implementation of the CIVICUS Monitor incident tracking tool Data entry and analysis of civic space violations Administrative and coordination support tasks Contribution to annual global reporting processes Requirements Bachelor’s degree (completed or near completion) in international relations, political science, or related field Strong written and spoken English Knowledge or experience in human rights research Understanding of international human rights frameworks (association, expression, peaceful assembly) Additional UN language proficiency is an advantage Application Requirements CV One-page motivation letter Applications must be original (AI-generated applications are not allowed) Deadline 8 July 2026, 11:59 PM SAST Start Date First week of August 2026 2. Data Coding Consultant – CIVICUS Monitor EU SEE Programme Overview of the Role This consultancy focuses on coding and structuring civic space data for analysis and reporting. Consultants will: Code civic space incidents from published CIVICUS Monitor updates Enter data into standardized civic space incident tools Support EU System for an Enabling Environment (EU SEE) data analysis Transform qualitative reports into structured datasets for trend analysis Contribute to early warning and civic space monitoring systems Requirements Bachelor’s degree or current student in international relations, political science, social sciences, or related fields Strong Excel and data analysis skills Experience in human rights research or monitoring preferred Strong English communication skills Ability to work independently and remotely High attention to detail and deadlines Application Requirements CV Cover letter detailing coding/data experience Proposed hourly and daily rates (USD or ZAR depending on location) Availability per week Deadline 8 July 2026 3. Civic Space Research Consultants (Regional Experts) Overview of the Role This consultancy supports drafting and editing CIVICUS Monitor country updates and briefs. Consultants will: Write and edit civic space country reports Analyze trends in human rights and civic freedoms Support monitoring of association, expression, and assembly rights Provide regional expertise (Africa, Asia, Latin America, Middle East, etc.) Contribute to ongoing global civic space documentation Requirements Master’s degree preferred (Political Science, Law, Sociology, International Relations, Journalism, etc.) Minimum 3 years of experience with civil society or human rights organizations Strong understanding of global civic space trends Regional language skills (French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, etc. depending on region) Strong writing and analytical skills in English Ability to work independently Application Requirements CV with writing samples Motivation letter specifying regional expertise Budget (hourly/daily rates in USD or ZAR) Deadline 8 July 2026 4. External End-of-Phase Evaluator (Netherlands MFA Partnership 2021–2026) Overview of the Role CIVICUS is seeking an independent evaluator or evaluation team to assess its strategic partnership with the Netherlands Ministry of Foreign Affairs under the Power of Voices framework. The evaluation covers 2021–2026 and includes: CIVICUS Monitor Membership and networks Advocacy campaigns (#StandAsMyWitness) Solidarity actions and global initiatives International Civil Society Week Key Responsibilities Inception phase design and evaluation framework development Mixed-methods data collection (interviews, surveys, case studies) Outcome harvesting and contribution analysis Global stakeholder engagement (especially Global South actors) Final evaluation report (max 60 pages + annexes) Requirements Experience evaluating international civil society organizations Knowledge of OECD/DAC or IOB evaluation criteria Expertise in qualitative evaluation methods Strong English communication skills (additional languages an advantage) Independence from CIVICUS-funded programs Deliverables Timeline Inception report: August 2026 Interim briefing: October 2026 Draft report: November 2026 Final report: January 2027 Deadline 13 July 2026, 23:59 SAST General Notes for All Opportunities All roles are remote or partially remote Strong focus on civic space, human rights, and civil society strengthening Preference for candidates with Global South representation High emphasis on independent, ethical, and non-AI-generated application materials (where applicable) Selected candidates may join CIVICUS preferred supplier lists for up to two years More global opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Global Career Opportunity for Young Professionals from Donor Countries
summary: The World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026 is now accepting applications for one of the organization s newest global talent initiatives designed to cultivate the next generation of development professionals. This prestigious two-year international programme offers young, high-potential professionals from eligible donor and developed countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on real-world development projects across World Bank Group country offices around the globe. Through an immersive, professionally supported experience, participants will contribute directly to development initiatives while expanding their global perspective, strengthening technical skills, and building careers dedicated to addressing some of the world s most pressing economic, environmental, and social challenges. Applications for the United Arab Emirates (UAE) donor cycle are currently open and will close on 15 July 2026 . This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the WBG Explorers Program 2026, including its objectives, programme features, eligibility requirements, available assignments, benefits, application process, and why it represents an outstanding career opportunity for aspiring international development professionals. What Is the WBG Explorers Program? The World Bank Group Explorers Program is an international talent programme created by the World Bank Group to connect outstanding young professionals from donor and developed countries with the organization s mission of ending extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity. The programme enables participants to gain direct exposure to World Bank Group operations by working on development projects across various sectors while living and working in WBG Country Offices around the world. Beyond professional development, the programme seeks to develop future leaders who will become lifelong advocates for international development and the World Bank Group s mission. Participants contribute meaningfully to projects while gaining valuable international experience that strengthens both technical expertise and leadership capacity. Objectives of the WBG Explorers Program The programme has several key objectives: Connect talented young professionals with the World Bank Group s development mission. Build future global development leaders. Provide practical experience working on international development projects. Strengthen understanding of global development challenges. Create ambassadors who will continue supporting sustainable development throughout their careers. Foster cross-cultural collaboration and international professional networks. Programme Features The WBG Explorers Program has been carefully designed to provide a structured and highly practical learning experience. Participants receive: Two-Year Extended Term Contract (ETC) Successful applicants receive a two-year Extended Term Contract (ETC) with the World Bank Group. The contract allows participants to gain substantial professional experience rather than short-term exposure, enabling them to make meaningful contributions to development operations. Country Office Placement Unlike many international programmes that are based at headquarters, WBG Explorers are placed directly within World Bank Group Country Offices around the world. Participants work alongside experienced professionals implementing projects where development challenges are being addressed firsthand. Headquarters-based placements are not included in this programme. Hands-on Development Work Explorers work on real projects aligned with their academic background, technical expertise, and professional interests. Assignments may involve: Economic analysis Infrastructure development Agriculture Water resource management Digital transformation Environmental sustainability Investment operations Artificial Intelligence Nature finance Inclusive conservation Financial analysis Policy development Regional development initiatives Participants contribute directly to ongoing World Bank Group operations while learning from experienced development practitioners. Mentorship Each Explorer is paired with a: Task Team Leader, or World Bank Group Manager Mentors provide: Professional guidance Technical supervision Career advice Performance feedback Opportunities for learning and growth This structured mentorship is one of the programme s major strengths. Cohort-Based Learning The programme follows a cohort model with annual recruitment. Participants benefit from: Customized onboarding Peer learning Cross-country collaboration Networking with fellow Explorers Shared professional development experiences Professional Growth The programme emphasizes continuous learning through: Exposure to multidisciplinary teams Collaboration across World Bank institutions Practical problem-solving International teamwork Global development operations Learn more about the program features HERE Funding and Benefits The WBG Explorers Program is fully funded through a donor-funded Trust Fund . Participants receive: Two-year paid employment contract Competitive salary Standard Extended Term Contract (ETC) benefits Structured mentorship International work experience Exposure to World Bank Group operations Professional development opportunities Global networking Hands-on experience solving development challenges Areas of Work Applicants can apply for opportunities across numerous sectors, including: Economic Policy Economics Finance Investment Agriculture Water Resources Land and Geospatial Digital Development Artificial Intelligence Integration Environmental and Social Development Business Administration Accounting Information Technology Nature Finance Inclusive Conservation These assignments allow participants to develop specialized expertise while contributing to sustainable development. Available Duty Stations The 2026 recruitment cycle includes opportunities in numerous World Bank Group Country Offices worldwide. Available locations include: Africa Johannesburg, South Africa Pretoria, South Africa Lagos, Nigeria Middle East Amman, Jordan Europe Belgrade, Serbia Bucharest, Romania Tirana, Albania Tbilisi, Georgia Latin America and the Caribbean Brasilia, Brazil São Paulo, Brazil Bogota, Colombia Buenos Aires, Argentina Panama City, Panama Santiago, Chile Multi-country Investment Positions Some investment roles may be based in: Johannesburg Lagos Istanbul Mumbai São Paulo Panama City depending on organizational needs. Available Fields Current opportunities include positions in: Economic Policy Land and Geospatial Digital Development Water Economics Agriculture AI Integration Nature Finance and Inclusive Conservation Investment Environmental and Social Development Finance Business Administration Accounting Information Technology Who Can Apply? The World Bank Group seeks talented individuals with strong academic backgrounds and a genuine interest in international development. Applicants should demonstrate: Intellectual curiosity Innovation Initiative Professionalism Commitment to development Strong communication skills Analytical thinking Teamwork Ability to manage multiple priorities International mobility Eligibility Criteria Applicants must satisfy the following requirements. Education Candidates must hold: A bachelor s degree. Professional Profile Applicants should demonstrate: Strong interest in development work Self-motivation Sense of urgency Innovation Intellectual curiosity Strong analytical ability Excellent written communication Strong verbal communication skills Ability to work under pressure Team collaboration skills Ability to manage multiple deadlines International mobility Nationality Applicants must be nationals of eligible donor or developed countries participating in the programme. Language Requirement Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in English. Learn more about the program eligibility HERE . Programme Timeline The programme follows an annual recruitment cycle. Typical schedule: Applications open annually Application review Candidate selection Onboarding around September Application Deadline For the 2026 UAE recruitment cycle , applications close on: 15 July 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. How to Apply Applications are submitted through the Official World Bank Group Careers Portal . Applicants should: Review available vacancies. Identify positions matching their qualifications. Prepare required application documents. Submit an online application before the deadline. Monitor communication regarding recruitment. Frequently Asked Questions Applicants often ask about: Programme objectives Eligibility requirements Contract duration Country office placements Benefits Mentorship Assignment process Project types Career progression Funding Application procedures The World Bank Group provides additional guidance through its official Frequently Asked Questions section . Why Apply for the WBG Explorers Program? The WBG Explorers Program provides far more than international work experience. Participants gain: Direct exposure to global development operations. Practical experience solving complex international challenges. Mentorship from experienced World Bank professionals. Experience working across cultures. Professional networks spanning multiple countries. Opportunities to strengthen technical and leadership skills. A deeper understanding of sustainable development. A strong foundation for future careers in international organizations, government, finance, consulting, and development institutions. For ambitious young professionals seeking meaningful international careers, the programme offers an exceptional pathway into one of the world s leading multilateral development organizations. Visit HERE for more information about the World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026. Conclusion The World Bank Group Explorers Program 2026 represents a unique opportunity for talented young professionals from eligible donor and developed countries to launch or accelerate a career in international development. Through a fully funded two-year Extended Term Contract, participants gain practical experience in World Bank Group Country Offices, receive mentorship from experienced leaders, collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, and contribute to projects that improve lives around the world. With placements available across Africa, Europe, Latin America, the Middle East, and other regions, the programme offers unparalleled exposure to global development challenges while helping participants build the technical expertise, leadership skills, and international networks needed for long-term success. Prospective applicants are encouraged to review the available positions carefully, identify the opportunities that best match their academic and professional background, and submit their applications before the 15 July 2026 deadline. Discover more global Job and Early Career Talent Program opportunities on OFY HERE . title: University of Calgary Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Political Science: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Canada
summary: The University of Calgary is inviting applications for the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science , an outstanding research opportunity for early-career scholars seeking to advance their academic careers at one of Canada s leading research-intensive universities. Hosted by the Department of Political Science within the Faculty of Arts , this prestigious fellowship provides postdoctoral researchers with the opportunity to conduct high-quality research, collaborate with renowned faculty members, contribute to teaching and academic life, and expand their professional networks in an internationally recognized academic environment. The fellowship is designed to support exceptional scholars who have recently completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue innovative research in political science. Through dedicated financial support, mentorship, and access to world-class research facilities, the program aims to foster academic excellence while preparing fellows for successful careers in higher education, research institutions, and public policy organizations. Application Deadline: Please refer to the official University of Calgary job posting for the closing date and submit your application before the position closes. About the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship The Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship honors the legacy of Mark Grosjean by supporting promising scholars whose research contributes to advancing political science. The Department of Political Science at the University of Calgary is recognized for producing impactful research across a wide range of political science disciplines, including comparative politics, Canadian politics, international relations, political theory, public policy, political economy, and quantitative political analysis. As part of the Faculty of Arts, fellows become members of an active academic community that encourages interdisciplinary collaboration, innovation, and public engagement. The fellowship provides an ideal platform for researchers to strengthen their publication record, develop new research projects, secure future grants, and establish themselves within the international political science community. What the Fellowship Offers Successful applicants will benefit from numerous professional and academic opportunities, including: A postdoctoral appointment at the University of Calgary. Access to world-class research facilities and academic resources. Collaboration with experienced faculty members and researchers. Opportunities to present research through seminars and conferences. Professional mentorship and career development. Participation in departmental academic activities. Opportunities to publish research in leading academic journals. An intellectually stimulating and collaborative research environment. The fellowship is designed not only to support independent research but also to encourage meaningful engagement with students, faculty members, and the wider academic community. Research Environment at the University of Calgary The University of Calgary consistently ranks among Canada s leading research universities. The Department of Political Science offers expertise across numerous research fields, providing fellows with opportunities to collaborate on interdisciplinary projects and contribute to research that informs public policy, governance, democratic institutions, and international affairs. Researchers benefit from: Modern research infrastructure. Extensive digital and physical library collections. Collaborative research centres. International research partnerships. Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration. Professional development resources. This environment allows fellows to pursue ambitious research agendas while developing valuable academic leadership skills. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is intended for outstanding early-career researchers who have recently completed a PhD in Political Science or a closely related discipline. Ideal applicants include scholars whose research interests align with areas such as: Comparative Politics Canadian Politics International Relations Public Policy Political Theory Political Economy Democratic Governance Electoral Politics Public Administration Quantitative Political Science Political Behaviour Applicants should demonstrate a strong record of academic achievement, high-quality research, publications, conference presentations, and the ability to contribute meaningfully to the department s research community. Why This Fellowship Matters Securing a postdoctoral fellowship is an important milestone in an academic career. Beyond providing dedicated time for research, postdoctoral appointments help scholars develop independent research portfolios, strengthen publication records, secure competitive grants, expand international collaborations, and prepare for faculty positions. The Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship offers researchers an opportunity to work within a supportive academic environment while contributing to research that addresses important political and societal challenges. Whether examining democratic governance, public institutions, global politics, or public policy, fellows are encouraged to produce research that advances knowledge and informs decision-making. Living and Working in Calgary Located in Alberta, Canada, Calgary is known for its high quality of life, vibrant research community, and strong academic institutions. Fellows benefit from living in a multicultural city with excellent infrastructure, access to nature, and numerous professional networking opportunities. The University of Calgary provides an inclusive and welcoming environment that values diversity, equity, and academic excellence, making it an attractive destination for researchers from around the world. How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare all required application documents as outlined in the official job advertisement . Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility requirements, required qualifications, and application instructions before submitting their materials. Applications should be submitted through the University of Calgary Careers portal . Application Deadline: Applicants should submit their applications before the closing date indicated on the official vacancy announcement. Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post University of Calgary Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Political Science: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: British Council Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call 2026: Funding Opportunity for International Technical and Vocational Education Partnerships
summary: The British Council has officially launched the Going Global Partnerships Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Grant Call 2026 , providing an exciting funding opportunity for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions seeking to establish or strengthen international partnerships. The grant programme is designed to support collaborative projects between eligible TVET institutions in the United Kingdom and partner countries. Through financial support and international cooperation, the initiative aims to improve the quality, relevance, and inclusiveness of vocational education while strengthening skills development that responds to labour market needs and contributes to sustainable economic growth. The Going Global Partnerships programme reflects the British Council s commitment to building stronger international education systems by encouraging knowledge exchange, institutional collaboration, innovation, and capacity building across borders. Application Deadline: Please refer to the official British Council grant page for the application closing date and country-specific deadlines. About the Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call Technical and Vocational Education and Training plays a critical role in preparing young people and adults with practical skills that improve employability, support entrepreneurship, and contribute to national development. Recognising the importance of quality skills education, the British Council developed the Going Global Partnerships programme to foster long-term collaboration between educational institutions, enabling them to learn from each other s expertise while addressing shared development challenges. The TVET Grant Call supports collaborative initiatives that strengthen institutional capacity, improve teaching and learning, modernize curricula, promote innovation, and enhance graduate employability. Rather than funding standalone projects, the programme focuses on building sustainable partnerships that continue generating impact long after the grant period has ended. Objectives of the Grant Programme The Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call aims to: Strengthen international institutional partnerships. Improve the quality of technical and vocational education. Enhance skills development aligned with labour market demands. Promote innovation in teaching and learning. Encourage curriculum development and modernization. Support knowledge exchange between institutions. Improve graduate employability. Foster inclusive and equitable access to quality TVET education. Build institutional resilience and long-term collaboration. These objectives contribute to broader efforts to equip learners with the technical and professional skills needed to succeed in rapidly changing economies. What Projects Can Be Supported? The grant encourages collaborative projects that may focus on areas such as: Curriculum review and development. Staff capacity building. Professional development for lecturers and instructors. Teaching and learning innovation. Industry engagement. Employer partnerships. Green skills. Digital skills. Entrepreneurship education. Gender equality and inclusion. Institutional leadership. Quality assurance. Research and policy development. Skills for emerging industries. Each partnership is expected to design activities that respond to local needs while promoting international best practices. Benefits of the Grant Successful institutions may benefit from: Grant funding for approved collaborative projects. International institutional partnerships. Access to global expertise. Knowledge sharing between partner institutions. Improved teaching capacity. Enhanced curriculum quality. Stronger industry engagement. Increased international visibility. Sustainable institutional collaboration. Better employment outcomes for students. The programme also helps participating institutions strengthen their international networks and create long-term strategic partnerships. Who Can Apply? The grant is intended for eligible Technical and Vocational Education and Training institutions participating in the Going Global Partnerships programme. Applications are generally submitted jointly by partner institutions, including: TVET colleges. Technical institutes. Vocational training institutions. Skills development centres. Further education institutions. Other eligible educational organizations as specified in the official call. Applicants should establish partnerships that demonstrate shared objectives, institutional commitment, and the potential to create lasting impact. Eligibility requirements vary depending on the participating country, and applicants should carefully review the official guidance before applying. Why This Grant Matters Across the world, employers increasingly require graduates equipped with practical, technical, digital, and entrepreneurial skills. Strong TVET systems help address unemployment, improve workforce productivity, encourage innovation, and support economic growth. Through international collaboration, institutions can exchange ideas, adopt successful practices, strengthen curricula, and better prepare learners for modern labour markets. The British Council s Going Global Partnerships programme helps institutions achieve these goals by supporting strategic partnerships that improve education quality while fostering international cooperation. How to Apply Interested institutions should review the official guidance provided by the British Council, identify an eligible partner institution where required, and prepare a joint project proposal addressing the programme objectives. Applications should demonstrate: Strong institutional partnerships. Clear project objectives. Sustainable impact. Capacity-building activities. Monitoring and evaluation plans. Alignment with programme priorities. Applicants are encouraged to submit well-developed proposals before the official deadline applicable to their country. For complete eligibility requirements, funding information, participating countries, and application guidance, visit the official British Council page: Discover global grants opportunities on OFY here The post British Council Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call 2026: Funding Opportunity for International Technical and Vocational Education Partnerships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: New York State Professional Pathways Program 2026: Paid Career Opportunities for Students and Recent Graduates
summary: The New York State Professional Pathways Program is now accepting applications from students and recent graduates who are eager to launch rewarding careers in public service. Administered by the New York State Department of Civil Service , this innovative employment initiative provides participants with paid opportunities to gain valuable professional experience while working in New York State government agencies. The Professional Pathways Program was created to attract talented individuals from diverse educational backgrounds and equip them with practical workplace experience, mentorship, and career development opportunities. Participants have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful public service projects while building the skills and experience needed for long-term careers in government and other professional sectors. Whether your interests lie in administration, communications, finance, information technology, engineering, environmental sciences, human resources, public policy, legal services, healthcare, or many other disciplines, the Professional Pathways Program offers a gateway to meaningful employment with New York State. Applications are accepted through available job postings, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible because vacancies may close once positions are filled. About the Professional Pathways Program The New York State Professional Pathways Program is designed to strengthen the state s workforce by recruiting talented students and recent graduates into public service careers. The program recognizes that many graduates face challenges obtaining professional experience immediately after completing their studies. Through structured employment opportunities, participants gain hands-on experience working alongside experienced professionals in state agencies while contributing to projects that improve services for New York residents. The initiative also supports workforce development by helping agencies recruit skilled professionals who can contribute innovative ideas, technical expertise, and fresh perspectives. Unlike traditional internships, many Professional Pathways positions provide competitive salaries, employee benefits where applicable, structured supervision, and opportunities for career advancement within New York State government. Benefits of Joining the Professional Pathways Program Participants enjoy numerous professional and career development benefits, including: Paid employment opportunities. Practical professional experience. Mentorship from experienced government professionals. Exposure to public administration and policymaking. Career development and professional networking. Opportunities to develop leadership and communication skills. Experience working on projects that benefit communities. Potential pathways to long-term employment within New York State government. Access to professional training and workplace learning. Opportunities across multiple career fields. The program allows participants to apply classroom knowledge to real-world challenges while strengthening their resumes and expanding their professional networks. Career Fields Available Professional Pathways positions may be available across a wide range of disciplines, including: Business Administration Accounting and Finance Economics Human Resources Information Technology Cybersecurity Data Analysis Communications Public Relations Marketing Public Policy Engineering Environmental Science Health Administration Legal Services Research Project Management Social Services Planning Operations Management Administrative Support Available positions vary throughout the year depending on agency needs. Who Can Apply? The program is intended for: Current undergraduate students. Graduate students. Recent graduates. Individuals seeking entry-level professional experience. Candidates interested in careers in public service. Applicants should demonstrate: Strong academic preparation. Good communication skills. Professionalism. Interest in serving the public. Ability to work collaboratively. Commitment to learning and professional growth. Individual vacancies may include additional educational or technical requirements depending on the role. Why Choose a Career in Public Service? Working in public service offers opportunities to make meaningful contributions that positively impact millions of people. Employees help develop policies, improve government services, manage public resources, strengthen communities, protect the environment, support education, improve transportation systems, advance healthcare, and contribute to economic development. The Professional Pathways Program introduces participants to these rewarding careers while providing valuable experience that can lead to permanent employment opportunities. Professional Development Opportunities Throughout their appointments, participants may receive opportunities to: Build technical skills. Improve leadership capabilities. Strengthen problem-solving skills. Develop teamwork and collaboration. Learn project management. Gain experience working with multidisciplinary teams. Understand government operations. Expand professional networks. These experiences prepare participants for successful careers both within and beyond government. Why This Program Stands Out The Professional Pathways Program combines professional employment, practical learning, mentorship, and career development in one comprehensive initiative. Rather than simply observing workplace activities, participants actively contribute to important government projects while developing transferable skills that employers highly value. The experience gained can significantly improve future employment prospects and provide a strong foundation for long-term career success. How to Apply Interested applicants should visit the official New York State Department of Civil Service Professional Pathways webpage to explore current vacancies and submit applications for positions that match their qualifications and career interests. Applications are submitted online through the official recruitment portal. Because hiring is based on available vacancies, applicants should monitor the website regularly for new opportunities. Application Deadline: There is no single application deadline. Positions are posted throughout the year, and each vacancy has its own closing date. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here The post New York State Professional Pathways Program 2026: Paid Career Opportunities for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits, Education, Healthcare, and Economic Development Organizations
summary: Organizations across the world are constantly searching for sustainable funding opportunities to scale their impact, expand their services, and improve the lives of the communities they serve. One of the most influential philanthropic organizations supporting transformative initiatives globally is the Michael Susan Dell Foundation. The Michael Susan Dell Foundation is dedicated to unlocking human potential and creating opportunities for children, families, and communities to thrive. Through strategic grant funding, the foundation partners with organizations that are working to solve some of society’s most pressing challenges in education, healthcare, livelihoods, financial inclusion, and community development. For organizations seeking grant funding in Africa, India, Israel, and the United States, the Michael Susan Dell Foundation Grants Program presents a significant opportunity to secure financial support and collaborate with a globally respected philanthropic institution. About the Michael Susan Dell Foundation Founded by Michael Dell and Susan Dell, the foundation focuses on creating pathways to opportunity for underserved populations. The organization invests in scalable, evidence-based solutions that help improve educational outcomes, enhance healthcare access, strengthen livelihoods, and promote economic mobility. Every year, the foundation contributes to: Supporting more than 500,000 classrooms with educational tools and resources. Helping approximately 350,000 students earn university degrees. Creating employment opportunities for over one million young adults. Expanding financial services to millions of entrepreneurs and families. Improving healthcare access and outcomes for millions of families. The foundation works through partnerships with organizations that demonstrate strong leadership, measurable impact, and the ability to scale solutions effectively. What the Foundation Funds The Michael Susan Dell Foundation provides grants to organizations working across several key focus areas. 1. Quality Schools The foundation supports initiatives designed to improve educational access and quality for students. This includes projects that strengthen schools, improve teaching practices, enhance student outcomes, and expand access to high-quality learning opportunities. 2. Classroom Supports Funding is available for programs that help teachers, school leaders, and educational systems deliver better learning outcomes. This may include educational technology, literacy interventions, data-driven instruction, curriculum development, and teacher training. 3. University Success The foundation invests in programs that help students enroll in, persist through, and graduate from higher education institutions. Projects that provide academic coaching, mentoring, student support services, and career readiness programs are particularly relevant. 4. Jobs and Livelihoods A major area of investment involves helping young people and adults gain skills, secure employment, and build sustainable livelihoods. The foundation funds workforce development programs, vocational training initiatives, apprenticeships, entrepreneurship support, and job placement programs. 5. Financial Services The foundation supports innovative approaches that improve financial inclusion and expand access to affordable financial products and services for underserved populations. 6. Health Innovation Healthcare organizations can receive funding for initiatives that improve maternal health, child health, healthcare delivery systems, digital health solutions, disease prevention, and access to quality medical care. 7. Community Development The foundation also invests in projects that strengthen communities, improve family well-being, and create long-term social and economic resilience. Regions Supported The Michael Susan Dell Foundation currently funds projects across multiple regions, including: Africa India Israel United States Texas-based initiatives Organizations operating within these regions are encouraged to explore potential funding opportunities that align with the foundation’s strategic priorities. Examples of Previously Funded Projects The foundation has funded hundreds of impactful initiatives across various sectors. Examples include: Youth employment and workforce development programs in Zambia. Healthcare innovations improving maternal and newborn health in East Africa. Educational initiatives supporting literacy and numeracy outcomes in African schools. Student success and graduation support programs in universities. Economic empowerment projects creating employment opportunities for young adults. Workforce training and apprenticeship programs that connect learners to industry needs. These investments demonstrate the foundation’s commitment to supporting scalable solutions that generate measurable outcomes. Benefits of Receiving a Michael Susan Dell Foundation Grant Organizations selected for funding may benefit from: Significant financial support for project implementation. Long-term strategic partnerships. Access to a global network of experts and organizations. Opportunities to scale successful interventions. Enhanced organizational credibility and visibility. Support for data-driven impact measurement and evaluation. Collaboration with experienced professionals focused on social impact. Who Should Apply? Organizations that have proven solutions addressing challenges in education, health, economic mobility, workforce development, financial inclusion, and community well-being should consider applying. Strong applicants typically demonstrate: A clear mission and measurable impact. Evidence-based interventions. Capacity to scale successful programs. Strong leadership and governance. Sustainable implementation strategies. Commitment to improving outcomes for underserved populations. How to Apply Organizations interested in securing funding can visit the Michael Susan Dell Foundation’s Grants Portal to learn more about funding priorities, eligibility requirements, and application procedures. Applicants should carefully review the foundation’s focus areas and ensure that their proposed projects align with the foundation’s mission of unlocking human opportunity and improving lives at scale. Since funding opportunities are available throughout the year, organizations are encouraged to monitor the foundation’s website regularly for updates and application guidance. Final Thoughts The Michael Susan Dell Foundation Grants Program remains one of the most impactful philanthropic funding opportunities available to organizations working in education, healthcare, economic development, and community transformation. Through its commitment to measurable impact and scalable solutions, the foundation continues to empower organizations that are creating lasting change for children, families, and communities around the world. For nonprofits, educational institutions, healthcare organizations, and social enterprises seeking substantial funding and strategic support, the Michael Susan Dell Foundation offers an opportunity to accelerate impact and contribute to a future where more people can access education, health, and economic opportunity. Deadline: There is currently no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here.
